_doc: Tool-safety-gate — cheap-model composition. A CHEAP haiku gate checks a proposed shell command BEFORE a destructive stage runs. The agent is parse:false and emits ONE keyword (SAFE/UNSAFE) — never JSON — so haiku's reliable JSON-mangling (BUG-697) can't break the gate. `extract` pulls the keyword into `safety`; `branch` routes SAFE -> approve (a stand-in for the real action), UNSAFE -> block. Fail-safe: a missing/garbled verdict becomes BLOCK and hits `default: block`, never approve. Scope honestly: a cheap model is competent at this PATTERN check (does the command match a destructive shape?), not at nuanced judgement.
